chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH BENCH AT SUKKUR C. P. No. S – 120 of 2025 Date of hearing Order with signature of Judge Hearing of case 1. For orders on office objection at Flag-A 2. For hearing of main case 20.06.2025 Mr. Farman Ali Shar, Advocate for petitioners. Mr. Gulzar Ahmed Malano, Assistant Prosecutor General. Mr. Shahryar Imdad Awan, Assistant Advocate General Sindh along with SIP Abdul Ghafoor of Police Station Mirpur Mathelo and SIP Hassan Ali Shar of Police Station Belo Mirpur..-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.- Statements have been filed on behalf of respondents No.2, 3 and 4, which are taken on record. The respondents have affirmed that they have neither harassed the petitioners nor do they intend to do so at any stage. It appears from the record that before approaching this Court, petitioner No.1 had filed Criminal Miscellaneous Application No.227 of 2025 before the learned Additional Sessions Judge-I / Ex-Officio Justice of Peace, Mirpur Mathelo. The said application was disposed of as dismissed, with ancillary directions issued by the learned Ex-Officio Justice of Peace. The relevant portion, i.e. Para No.6 of the said order, reads as under: “However, as long as the petitioner remain within the jurisdiction of Taluka Mirpur Mathelo, the SHOs PS Bero Mirpur, PS Mirpur Mathelo, and the SSP Ghotki @ Mirpur Mathelo are directed to ensure their adequate protection and address any issues of harassment and protection, whenever the petitioner approach the police for that purpose, until she appear before the competent court for the redressal of her grievances.” In light of the above and the statements filed by the official respondents, the official respondents are once again directed to act strictly

The relevant portion, i.e. Para No.6 of the said order, reads as under: “However, as long as the petitioner remain within the jurisdiction of Taluka Mirpur Mathelo, the SHOs PS Bero Mirpur, PS Mirpur Mathelo, and the SSP Ghotki @ Mirpur Mathelo are directed to ensure their adequate protection and address any issues of harassment and protection, whenever the petitioner approach the police for that purpose, until she appear before the competent court for the redressal of her grievances.” In light of the above and the statements filed by the official respondents, the official respondents are once again directed to act strictly C. P. No. S – 120 of 2025 Page 2 of 2 in accordance with law and ensure that no harassment is caused to the petitioners. If the petitioners approach the concerned police for protection or redressal of any grievance, the same shall be dealt with in accordance with law. The petition stands disposed of in the above terms. J U D G E Abdul Basit
